isolation.notes: Validated only the assigned assertions against the already-running local Next.js app; the server was not restarted.

assertions.title: Liquid metal card finish with glass-morphism

steps.action: Open the root page in a headed browser session and inspect graph-workspace cards and lower proof/content cards at rest.
steps.expected: Cards across graph and content surfaces should read as translucent glass panels with subtle white borders and a premium cohesive finish.
steps.observed: Both atlas graph cards and lower proof cards rendered as transparent glass panels with the blue shader clearly visible behind them; sampled graph and proof cards shared the same 0.04 white background, 0.12 white border, and 8px radius.

steps.action: Hover a graph card and capture the interaction state.
steps.expected: Hover should show a visible metallic sheen or radial highlight, not only a flat opacity change.
steps.observed: The hover capture showed a bright metallic hotspot over the atlas card surface, creating a clear liquid-metal sheen effect that read as intentional and premium.

steps.action: Cross-check the shipped card implementation for the hover finish.
steps.expected: The implementation should define a transparent glass base with a stronger radial metallic center highlight on hover.
steps.observed: `src/components/LiquidMetalCard.tsx` lines 127-134 define a 0.04 base, 0.08 hover background, 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12) border, and a radial-gradient sheen with a center highlight of rgba(255,255,255,0.38).

evidence.network: No requests captured in this local visual-only validation flow.
codeAudit: `src/components/LiquidMetalCard.tsx:127-134` -> base background rgba(255,255,255,0.04), hover background rgba(255,255,255,0.08), radial-gradient center rgba(255,255,255,0.38), border rgba(255,255,255,0.12).

assertions.title: All text is white — no grey text

steps.action: Inspect visible text in the top shell and in a lower proof/content surface and collect computed styles.
steps.expected: All rendered text should be white (#ffffff), with hierarchy coming from weight/size/opacity rather than grey colors.
steps.observed: The body, hero tagline, proof heading, lower proof paragraph, and footer text all computed to rgb(255,255,255). Secondary sampled text used white with opacity (`oklab(... / 0.7)` and `oklab(... / 0.8)`), which is opacity-based hierarchy rather than grey.

steps.action: Check visible CTA/button text styles on the same page.
steps.expected: CTA text should also remain white if every text element on the site must be white.
steps.observed: The primary CTA `See What's Live` computed to rgb(16,16,16) dark text on a white button background, and the same dark-on-white treatment is visible on lower white CTA buttons.

steps.action: Run code-audit greps for prohibited grey text utility classes in component className props.
steps.expected: `text-secondary`, `text-[#a0a0a0]`, `text-[#707070]`, and `text-muted` should not appear in component className props.
steps.observed: Grep across `src/components` returned no matching files for `text-secondary`, `text-[#a0a0a0]`, `text-[#707070]`, or `text-muted`; `src/app/globals.css` also sets `--text-secondary: #ffffff`.

evidence.network: No requests captured in this local visual-only validation flow.
codeAudit: `src/components` grep for `text-secondary` -> no matching files found.
`src/components` grep for `text-[#a0a0a0]` -> no matching files found.
`src/components` grep for `text-[#707070]` -> no matching files found.
`src/components` grep for `text-muted` -> no matching files found.

assertions.issues: The strict assertion fails because at least the primary and lower white CTA buttons render dark text (`rgb(16,16,16)`) instead of white, even though sampled body, hero, proof, and footer text are white and no prohibited grey text utility classes were found in component className props.

assertions.title: Liquid metal cards complement shader background

steps.action: Inspect graph and proof cards in the default state over the live shader background.
steps.expected: Cards should use nearly transparent glass so the shader remains visible through them, avoiding opaque-box surfaces.
steps.observed: Default atlas cards and lower proof cards allowed the blue shader texture to remain clearly visible through the panels; they read as floating glass layers rather than opaque boxes.

steps.action: Capture a hover state and compare it with the default card state.
steps.expected: Hover should add a clearly visible metallic sheen with a strong center highlight while keeping the card transparent overall.
steps.observed: The hover capture showed a pronounced radial highlight across the hovered atlas card while the surrounding shader remained visible, preserving the transparent glass feel.

steps.action: Cross-check runtime and code evidence for the card opacity contract.
steps.expected: Rendered cards should resolve to the requested low-opacity base/border values, and the shipped hover sheen should meet or exceed a 30% center opacity target.
steps.observed: Runtime computed styles showed graph and proof cards at rgba(255,255,255,0.04) with rgba(255,255,255,0.12) borders; code audit in `LiquidMetalCard.tsx` sets the hover sheen center to rgba(255,255,255,0.38), exceeding the 30% requirement.

evidence.network: No requests captured in this local visual-only validation flow.
codeAudit: `src/components/LiquidMetalCard.tsx:127-134` -> base 0.04, hover 0.08, border 0.12, hover sheen center 0.38.

summary: Tested 3 assigned readability visual assertions. VAL-VISUAL-030 and VAL-VISUAL-035 passed: cards render as near-transparent liquid-metal glass panels with the shader visible through them and a clearly visible hover sheen. VAL-VISUAL-034 failed because sampled body/hero/proof/footer text is white and no prohibited grey utility classes were found, but white CTA buttons still render dark text instead of white.
